name: Recovery Disabled in Boot Configuration Data
notes:
This rule triggers when recovery options are disabled in the Boot Configuration Data.
This could indicate a malicious actor attempting to disable to ability to recover files from a ransomware attack.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES 'bcdedit\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '.*?\/set\s+recoveryenabled\s+no.*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Recovery Disabled in Boot Configuration Data on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES 'bcdedit\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") MATCHES '.*?\/set\s+recoveryenabled\s+no.*' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Recovery Disabled in Boot Options
Event description: Recovery Boot options has been disabled on a machine.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
